plants.name: pothos / trailing vine
plants.current_state: generally healthy overall, but sprawling and likely to get messy if left in the same position with one-size-fits-all care
plants.priority_issue: trailing growth and mixed light exposure matter more than any severe visible damage
plants.next_step: give it bright indirect light, rotate it occasionally, and trim / redirect the longest growth if it starts to crowd the desk area

plants.name: snake plant
plants.priority_issue: watering all plants on the same weekly schedule is likely too frequent for this plant
plants.next_step: let the soil dry more between waterings and avoid overwatering

plants.name: small rosette succulent
plants.current_state: compact and generally healthy-looking, but still the plant most likely to need the brightest light and the lightest watering hand
plants.priority_issue: it can decline quickly if treated like the other plants under the same weekly watering routine
plants.next_step: keep it in the brightest sill, rotate it occasionally, and let the soil dry much more fully between waterings

environmental_factors: same watering amount for all pots is a bad fit
AC and desk placement may dry leaves or keep some plants farther from ideal light
uncertainty_note: The current pothos images look healthier than an obviously drooping rescue case. Accept healthy-but-sprawling or mildly untidy readings; do not require strong distress language.
